event.summary: Reviewed the bounded proposal and made it claimable without expanding its repositories, paths, or prerequisites.

acceptance.proof: XmlElementSerializationLimit rejects zero and exposes only an explicit maximum output byte count. XmlElementSerialization::serialize borrows an accepted XmlElementTree and produces private accepted state containing exactly the selected limit and one owned String. Borrowed and consuming output access preserve exact UTF-8 content and the output allocation; the source tree remains available and unchanged.

The fixed local policy emits every node as an explicit start tag and end tag, emits no self-closing form or inserted whitespace, and emits accepted XmlCharacterData verbatim between leaf tags. Containers emit their direct children depth-first; siblings are selected by ascending original arena index even when parent-before-child arena order is not preorder. Traversal is iterative with one fallibly reserved bounded stack and no recursion.

Exact UTF-8 output length is calculated with checked arithmetic before allocation. Stable rejection order is indexed length overflow, exact output-byte limit, one exact fallible traversal-stack reservation, then one exact fallible output-String reservation. Typed errors retain the failing node or exact expected, required, and requested counts, and the output length equals the accepted precomputed value.

Focused tests cover leaf roots, empty containers, nested and non-preorder arenas, siblings, Unicode and colon names, deep accepted trees, exact and one-past byte limits, allocation helpers, and a CORE-004 to LSLC-001F to LSLC-001G path. Existing ampersand, less-than, and greater-than character references from accepted XmlCharacterData are emitted exactly once without decoding, revalidation, re-escaping, normalization, or source allocation replacement.

A separate independently authored LSLC-001G local-results overlay binds only to accepted LSLC-001B through LSLC-001F contracts. Every LSLC-001A oracle and candidate role remains not-observed with null evidence; tag spelling, empty-container spelling, hierarchy traversal, and output bytes remain local candidate policy with no stream-info, info, desc, endpoint, official-liblsl, round-trip, wire, or compatibility meaning.

The crate remains dependency-, feature-, unsafe-, I/O-, activation-, parser-, decoder-, protocol-, wire-, runtime-, FFI-, adapter-, provider-, and device-free; historical evidence remains unchanged and the feature/effect lock remains empty and inert.

document.commit_policy: Commit the bounded local XmlElementTree serializer separately from StreamDefinition or stream-info field mapping, XML declarations or general document production, parsing or decoding, oracle capture, protocol, transport, adapters, providers, or runtime work.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the borrowed bounded serializer, exact local spelling and hierarchy order, allocation precedence, and strict no-LSL-document/no-parser/no-runtime boundary.
Describe the local iterative element-tree string policy, byte and ownership bounds, composition path, and the absence of stream-info, endpoint, protocol, wire, I/O, or runtime meaning.
Review whether bounded borrowed serialization adds reusable allocation, derived-view, or evidence guidance; retain the installed skill unchanged if its current rules are sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the clean-source, public-evidence, and future LSL adapter boundary for local element serialization; retain the installed router unchanged if its current rules cover the unit.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: XML declaration, byte-order mark, encodings other than UTF-8, self-closing tags, pretty printing, inserted whitespace, attributes, namespaces, mixed content, CDATA sections, comments, processing instructions, schemas, queries, entity selection beyond already accepted XmlCharacterData, or general XML DOM behavior.
Parsing, character-reference or entity decoding, logical-text recovery, canonicalization, mutation, source consumption, bidirectional conversion, round-trip claims, or hidden output limits.

StreamDefinition or StreamDescriptor projection, core field names, numeric or channel-format spelling, channels, info or desc roles, stream-info or complete LSL document meaning, LSL field mapping, endpoint representation, or exact liblsl output.

Oracle capture, candidate differential comparison, official-liblsl behavior or compatibility claims, protocol versions, wire framing, discovery, resolution, networking, sockets, inlets, outlets, transport, buffering, queues, threads, clocks, recovery, providers, FFI, native loading, adapters, language bindings, filesystem or standard-stream I/O.

Manifold stream authority, commands, sessions, streams, topology, identity, permission, admission, leases, revisions, audit, platform lifecycle, Cargo dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, additional crates, generated protocol material, copied or translated liblsl or rLSL source, device validation, or runtime activation.
document.objective: Implement one dependency-free, borrowed, explicitly byte-bounded, fallible, non-recursive serialization of an accepted XmlElementTree into an owned UTF-8 String under fixed local element spelling and hierarchy-order policy, preserving accepted names and represented character data without decoding or re-escaping and without adding StreamDefinition mapping, LSL document roles, oracle claims, protocol, wire, I/O, or runtime behavior.
outputs: A private xml_element_serialization module with public XmlElementSerializationLimit, XmlElementSerialization, and XmlElementSerializationError APIs that borrow an accepted XmlElementTree and return one bounded owned UTF-8 String without changing the source.

Positive and damaged tests proving exact local tag spelling, hierarchy traversal with sibling arena-index order, represented-character-data preservation, non-recursive bounded scratch use, checked byte accounting, deterministic limit and allocation errors, exact output allocation, source preservation, and CORE-004 through LSLC-001G composition.

A separate LSLC-001G local-results overlay, deterministic focused checker, aggregate-gate routing, and public documentation that labels all spelling and traversal choices as local policy while preserving corpus, oracle, LSL-mapping, protocol, wire, I/O, runtime, and authority boundaries.
